title: Ardisia sordida

This family is a part of the order Ericales, a large order of flowering plants that includes many woody and herbaceous species. The species Ardisia sordida is further classified as a member of the genus Ardisia, a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Ardisia is divided into several species, including Ardisia sordida, which is native to Southeast Asia. The classification of Ardisia sordida reflects its evolutionary relationships with other species of flowering plants.

The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antidiabetic, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to lower blood sugar levels and reduce oxidative stress.
